other_observers: Marcel, Joel, Renee, and Dave Such, Walter Szeliga. Seen by 100 or so birders on 9 Jan. on the ABA/CFO/DFO/BBC trip.; First reported by Ted Floyd on 26 December.
description: Very large, dark immature gull. Nearly a head taller than nearby Herring Gulls. Bill black and heavy. Head whitish, contrasted strongly with blackish mantle. Overall lack of brown tones, mantle dark blackish-gray with very clean checkering of white. Blackish primaries, relatively short primary projection. Pink legs.Behaviors: Roosting with a mixed flock of gulls on ice.Call: not heardPlumage: 1st Winter
